source_note: Original TOEIC-style workplace simulation content generated for this application. Not official test-provider material.

speaking.title: Read Aloud 1
speaking.prompt_text: Good morning, everyone. The executive briefing room on the fourth floor will be unavailable on July 11 while the facilities team recalibrates the hybrid presentation system and replaces several ceiling microphones. Please redirect scheduled meetings to Conference Room B and notify external guests in advance.
speaking.sample_response: Good morning, everyone. The executive briefing room on the fourth floor will be unavailable on July 11 while the facilities team recalibrates the hybrid presentation system and replaces several ceiling microphones. Please redirect scheduled meetings to Conference Room B and notify external guests in advance.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_note: No prompt audio: this TOEIC SW task uses on-screen text or picture stimulus.

speaking.title: Read Aloud 2
speaking.prompt_text: Thank you for calling Northbridge Analytics. Our service desk is open from eight thirty in the morning until six in the evening, Monday through Friday. For urgent delivery discrepancies, please leave your reference number, destination branch, and preferred callback time after the tone.
speaking.sample_response: Thank you for calling Northbridge Analytics. Our service desk is open from eight thirty in the morning until six in the evening, Monday through Friday. For urgent delivery discrepancies, please leave your reference number, destination branch, and preferred callback time after the tone.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_note: No prompt audio: this TOEIC SW task uses on-screen text or picture stimulus.

speaking.title: Describe Picture 3
speaking.prompt_text: Describe the picture on the screen in as much detail as possible.
speaking.sample_response: The picture shows a cross-functional team reviewing operational documents in a modern office area. One employee appears to be clarifying data on a shared screen while another compares printed materials, suggesting that they are preparing for a detailed business discussion.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_note: No prompt audio: this TOEIC SW task uses on-screen text or picture stimulus.

speaking.title: Respond to Question 5
speaking.prompt_text: What steps do you take when a customer or colleague gives you incomplete information?
speaking.sample_response: A strong response answers directly, adds a specific reason, and uses clear workplace vocabulary.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

speaking.title: Respond to Question 7
speaking.prompt_text: Should companies allow teams to reject urgent requests when those requests create quality risks? Give reasons and examples.
speaking.sample_response: A strong response answers directly, adds a specific reason, and uses clear workplace vocabulary.

speaking.title: Use Provided Information 8
speaking.prompt_text: What activity begins at ten o clock?
speaking.information_card:
Finance Systems Migration Briefing
Organizer: Northbridge Analytics finance office
9:15-9:45 Identity verification and temporary device setup
10:00-10:50 Legacy-report mapping in Room 6
11:05-12:00 Exception approval workflow demonstration
13:10-14:00 Audit-trail validation workshop
14:20-15:15 Department rollout timeline and help-desk routing
speaking.sample_response: A strong response uses only the schedule information and answers the caller clearly.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

speaking.title: Express an Opinion
speaking.prompt_text: Some organizations measure productivity by the number of tasks completed, while others measure it by the quality and durability of outcomes. Which method is better for professional work? Give reasons and examples.
speaking.sample_response: A strong response states a clear preference, gives two developed reasons, and uses a concrete workplace example.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_loudness: loud literal TTS with provider-specific normalization
speaking.audio_script:
Directions: Express your opinion and support it with reasons and examples.

Some organizations measure productivity by the number of tasks completed, while others measure it by the quality and durability of outcomes. Which method is better for professional work? Give reasons and examples.
speaking.audio_transcript:
Directions: Express your opinion and support it with reasons and examples.

writing.title: Picture Sentence 1
writing.prompt_text: Write one sentence based on the picture. Use both required words or phrases. You may change the form of the words and use them in any order.
writing.sample_response: The manager is reviewing the updated schedule with her team.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For writing, include sentence accuracy, content coverage, tone, and development.

writing.title: Respond to a client request
writing.prompt_text: You received an email from a client who wants to move a product demonstration from July 11 to the following week because their procurement committee added a compliance review. Write a response that acknowledges the request, explains what you need to check, and offers a practical next step.
writing.sample_response: A strong email response addresses every requested point, uses a professional tone, and ends with a clear next step.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

writing.title: Respond to an internal request
writing.prompt_text: Your manager has asked whether the training team can prepare a concise update for next Friday's staff meeting after several regional targets were revised. Write a response that confirms availability, mentions one topic you will include, and asks one practical question.
writing.sample_response: A strong email response addresses every requested point, uses a professional tone, and ends with a clear next step.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

writing.title: Opinion Essay
writing.prompt_text: Some people believe that employees learn best by working with experienced coworkers. Others believe formal training courses are more effective because they create consistent standards across a company. Which view do you agree with? Give reasons and examples to support your opinion.
writing.sample_response: A strong essay states a clear position, develops at least two reasons, includes workplace examples, and uses organized paragraphs.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For writing, include sentence accuracy, content coverage, tone, and development.
